hook_check.note: banner must reference the in-progress todo bookmark as the source of 'step'
Case A (dict + entry None + non-empty valid ids): likely-cause + advance-todo remediation present

hook_check.no_usable_step_ids_directs: do not retry by re-bookmarking; re-publish the contract / request orchestrator escalation

negative_assertions.note: On Case M (non-dict contract) and Case C (dict with empty/non-list/missing required_calls) the composed banner MUST NOT leak the AFFIRMATIVE stale-bookmark remediation (the Case A action that tells the agent to advance/mark a todo step to in_progress) — it must direct malformed/unpublished-contract handling instead. POLARITY-AWARE: a Case M/C banner is allowed (indeed expected) to use the PROHIBITIVE phrase 'do not retry by re-bookmarking' — that negative phrase is NOT a leak. The assertion therefore targets the affirmative-directive co-occurrence, NOT raw tokens. (Codex ba-iter3 ISSUE 1: a raw {advance,mark,in_progress,bookmark} denylist self-contradicts, because a compliant 'do NOT re-bookmark' Case M/C banner contains the substrings 'bookmark' and 'mark'.)
negative_assertions.must_not_contain_affirmative_advance_dir: the banner MUST NOT contain an affirmative directive to advance/mark a todo to in_progress — concretely, it MUST NOT contain BOTH 'advance' AND 'in_progress' (the Case A remediation signature). A prohibitive 'do not re-bookmark' phrase is explicitly allowed.

hook_check.derivation: contract_runtime._iter_required_calls(contract); keep only dict entries with non-empty str 'step'; dedupe via seen-set in contract order; join ', '
hook_check.order_preservation_discriminator: fixtures (ii) and (iii) have contract order != sorted-dedup order; a sorted implementation produces a DIFFERENT exact string and therefore FAILS — this is the assertion the prior single dedup fixture ['2a','8','2a']->'2a, 8' could not make (it is byte-identical under both orderings because '2a' < '8' lexically)

fixtures.divergent: sorted-dedup would wrongly produce '2a, 8'; order-preserving produces '8, 2a'
sorted-dedup would wrongly produce '10, 11g, 2'; order-preserving deduped produces '11g, 2, 10'

hook_check.verification: component-level: invoke the derivation/banner helper with these fixtures; assert exact equality on each. Fixtures (ii) and (iii) MUST fail a sorted implementation. Recursive /dev|/close|/commit invocation forbidden.

hook_check.caseM: not isinstance(contract, dict) => malformed-contract diagnosis (re-publish/escalate, do NOT re-bookmark)
hook_check.caseC: isinstance(contract,dict) and valid_contracted_step_ids==[] and entry is None => incomplete-contract diagnosis (re-publish/escalate)
hook_check.caseA: isinstance(contract,dict) and valid_contracted_step_ids!=[] and entry is None => stale-bookmark diagnosis (advance todo)
hook_check.caseB: entry present => role/mode/pipeline mismatch diagnosis + expected_for_resolved_step
hook_check.discriminator: isinstance(contract, dict) then valid_contracted_step_ids emptiness then entry presence — NOT error-string content

negative_assertions.note: Positive case-label presence is necessary but NOT sufficient: a Case M/C banner must also NOT leak the Case A AFFIRMATIVE stale-bookmark remediation (advance/mark a todo to in_progress). POLARITY-AWARE assertion: the Case M/C diagnosis is EXPECTED to use the prohibitive phrase 'do not retry by re-bookmarking', so raw tokens 'mark'/'bookmark' (which appear inside 're-bookmarking') and 'in_progress' alone are NOT leaks. The leak signature is the affirmative co-occurrence 'advance' AND 'in_progress'. (Codex ba-iter3 ISSUE 1: a raw {advance,mark,in_progress,bookmark} denylist self-contradicts the AC5 positive Case M wording which itself contains 'bookmark'/'mark'.)
negative_assertions.caseM_caseC_must_NOT_contain_affirmative: MUST NOT contain BOTH 'advance' AND 'in_progress' (the Case A remediation signature); the prohibitive 'do not re-bookmark' phrase is explicitly allowed
negative_assertions.caseA_contrast_fixture: contract={'required_calls':[{'step':'2a'},{'step':'8'}]} with resolved step absent => banner MUST contain the affirmative advance remediation (BOTH 'advance' AND 'in_progress'), proving Case A and Case M/C diverge on the affirmative-directive signature

hook_check.invariant: exit(2) iff not result['ok'] and result['severity']=='fail'
hook_check.component_check: invoke validate_required_call in isolation for no-entry/role-mismatch/mode-mismatch/clean-match/non-dict-contract; severity+ok+entry-presence identical pre/post

hook_check.behavioral_assert: no exception raised AND valid_contracted_step_ids == '<none>' for EVERY fixture above. NOTE (Codex ba-iter3 ISSUE 2): rendering '<none>' on non-dicts proves only GUARDED/non-crashing behavior — it does NOT by itself prove the EXACT accessor was used (an impl could inline isinstance, special-case non-dicts, or catch TypeError). Exact-accessor use is therefore asserted at the source level below, not inferred from this fixture.
hook_check.source_level_assert: static check of hooks/pretool-subagent-enforce.py: (a) the step-id derivation calls/reuses contract_runtime._iter_required_calls (the mandated isinstance-guarded accessor, contract_runtime.py:244-249); (b) NO direct subscript contract['required_calls'] appears in the banner/derivation path; (c) `git diff --quiet hooks/lib/contract_runtime.py` (contract_runtime.py byte-unchanged). These three together prove the accessor mandate (ticket M7 / Dev constraint OBJ2), which the black-box fixture alone cannot.
hook_check.forbidden: direct subscript contract['required_calls'] (raises TypeError on non-dict)
